notes.anchor: let us by all means see the gardens through
notes.note: Cicero's protracted hunt to buy suburban pleasure-gardens (horti) across the Tiber, intended as a shrine (fanum) for his dead daughter Tullia; the negotiations recur throughout books 12-13.

notes.anchor: decidedly clever little things --- addressed to Varro
notes.note: The four books of the recast Academica, now dedicated to Varro. Cicero frets over the dedication --- whether Varro really wants it, and whether he will be jealous of the company he keeps among Cicero's dialogue figures.
